summary: Base64 decode pattern in web3-token-helper requires manual review
details: The package's index.js (around line 140) contains a Buffer.from(..., 'base64').toString(...) decoding pattern. This idiom by itself is not evidence of malicious behavior — base64 decode is widely used for legitimate purposes (JWT parsing, encoded asset embedding, signature handling in web3 libraries). However, the code path was not traced end-to-end, so it cannot be confirmed whether the decoded bytes are passed to an interpreter, eval'd, written to disk, or sent over the network. Given that the package name suggests a web3 token utility (a domain where caller-supplied secrets like wallet keys / signed messages may transit through helper functions), a human reviewer should inspect the decode site to confirm the decoded data is data-shape (parsed as a token, JSON, etc.) and not passed to eval / Function / vm.runIn* / child_process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
